meta.phase-name: Visual composer foundations and builder shell

highlights.item: Phase 15 is now closed with a documented block audit and registry/state contract grounded in the current Payload page layout and Grime Time renderers.
The composer now has a formal block registry with `dynamic`, `static`, and `container` categories plus a visible `Custom HTML` taxonomy entry.
The builder header owns page-level title, slug, working page, draft creation, visibility, route preview, and publish controls without repeating page identity inside Structure.

The Structure tab now behaves like a real block organizer: ordered rows, add-below, duplicate, delete, hide/show, drag reorder, and insertion targets that open the block library at the exact target index.

Phase 15 adds persisted block visibility across the current page-layout block tables, keeps hidden blocks visible to editors with badges, and omits them from public rendering through a shared visibility helper.
The categorized block browser now inserts real Grime Time block definitions instead of ad hoc templates and keeps the registry aligned with existing production blocks.
Verification now covers the builder foundations through focused integration tests, production typecheck/build, and browser verification against a real `next start` surface.

next.item: The global project focus remains phase `14`, task `14-02` for quote-estimator pricing calibration.
When composer work resumes, phase `16` is the next stream: mirrored selection, hover actions, inline editing, responsive preview modes, and publish workflow refinement.
